rationale.root_cause: In the bad function, the copy loop uses destLen (length of dest ≈99) as the bound while data points to the 50-byte dataBadBuffer, causing reads past the end of the source buffer. This precisely explains the buffer over-read at the loop site where dest[i] = data[i] is executed with i >= source length.
rationale.local_fix: They bound the copy by the actual source length (i < src_len) rather than destLen, preventing reading past data’s buffer. This directly addresses the immediate over-read in the loop.
rationale.global_fix: They recommend using bounded-copy APIs (e.g., strncpy_s/memcpy_s or similar) and abstractions that pass explicit lengths, avoiding reliance on NUL-termination. This addresses the systemic issue by carrying and enforcing sizes through the API.
